## 3. Deep Research panel

**Lifetime DR review verdicts**

| Verdict | n | of 408 |
|---|---:|---:|
| (no DR run) | 335 | 82.1% |
| ADJUSTED | 41 | 10.0% |
| OVERRIDDEN | 26 | 6.4% |
| ERROR_PARSING | 3 | 0.7% |
| UPGRADED | 1 | 0.2% |
| INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS | 1 | 0.2% |
| CONFIRMED | 1 | 0.2% |

Of the 73 rows that *did* reach DR: ADJUSTED 56.2% · OVERRIDDEN 35.6% · ERROR/INCOMPLETE 5.5% · CONFIRMED/UPGRADED 2.7%. DR's override rate on PM **BUY** specifically is **19/56 = 33.9%** lifetime — DR is killing roughly one in three PM BUYs, which is a real, load-bearing filter, not cosmetic. On **BUY_LIMIT** the override rate is only 7/56 = 12.5%, but that's largely an artifact of weak coverage (see §6).

**DR action distribution (lifetime, the 73 that ran):** BUY_LIMIT 32 · AVOID 29 · BUY 11 · WATCH 1.

**Today's DR individual cases (2 of 12 went to DR):**

| ID | Ticker | PM | DR verdict | DR action | Δ | DR reasoning (excerpt) |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 403 | ACM | BUY | ADJUSTED | BUY | same | "98% operating cash flow collapse was a transient Q2 timing issue tied to Middle East payment delays … combined with raised full-year EPS guidance, record 16.5% margins …" |
| 409 | ONON | BUY | **OVERRIDDEN** | AVOID | **PM→AVOID** | "Despite robust trailing margins, structural wholesale deceleration, massive insider selling, and major governance red flags make this technical dip a dangerous value trap rather than a buying opportunity." |

ONON is exactly the type of save the senior reviewer is meant to deliver — governance + insider selling caught after PM saw a clean drop. ACM passed cleanly and is the only candidate today that became a live position.

🚨 **WTW**: current price (249.61) is *below* the stop_loss_price (253.50). The stop is also above the entry (248.99) — meaning either (a) this is a trailing-stop that was raised above entry and is now breached, or (b) the stop levels were inverted on entry. In either case the system has not exited yet despite the trigger condition. Treat as a HIGH-severity flag (see §6) and verify by hand before next session.

## 5. Today's full screening haul

Sector data is **NULL for all 12** of today's rows (a `decision_points.sector` is unpopulated — see §6 MEDIUM flag), so the grouping below is inferred from `company_name`.

**Healthcare / pharma (3)** — RGC (Regencell Bioscience), HIMS (Hims & Hers), RDY (Dr. Reddy's). All three → PM **AVOID**, no DR.

**Tech / hardware / cloud (5)** — UI (Ubiquiti), CAMT (Camtek), CRWV (CoreWeave), FORM (FormFactor), ACM (AECOM is engineering, not tech, but routed here in the spec sense — see industrials).
- UI → AVOID, CRWV → AVOID, CAMT → WATCH, FORM → WATCH. All four skipped DR.

**Industrials / engineering (1)** — **ACM (AECOM)** → PM **BUY** → DR **ADJUSTED → BUY** → **Owned**. The single survivor.

**Consumer / discretionary (2)** — W (Wayfair) → AVOID; **ONON (On Holding)** → PM **BUY** → DR **OVERRIDDEN → AVOID**. DR's kill of the day.

**Communications / telecom (1)** — VOD (Vodafone) → AVOID, no DR.

**Aerospace / satellite (1)** — ASTS (AST SpaceMobile) → AVOID, no DR.

| Outcome | Today |
|---|---:|
| Became BUY/BUY_LIMIT | 2 (ACM, ONON) |
| Killed by DR | 1 (ONON) |
| Became live position | 1 (ACM) |
| Stuck PENDING | 0 today |
| Total candidates | 12 |

Gatekeeper tier: **11 DEEP_DIP, 1 STANDARD_DIP** (ASTS). The funnel is dominated by deep-dip candidates today.

ACM (id 403) is flagged `decision_points.status = 'Owned'` but **no matching `desk_positions` row has been written yet**. The desk-position writer normally runs in a different tick — re-check tomorrow morning; if still missing, the writer is broken.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

| Severity | Flag | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| 🔴 **HIGH** | `decision_tracking` is **empty** (0 rows, 0 unique decisions) | Tracking service either never started in this period or is writing to a different store. Post-decision price movement is therefore impossible to evaluate from this DB. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **0 subscribers** | `subscribers` table is empty; the daily email summary background task has no recipients. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **WTW position breached stop** but is still ACTIVE | current_price 249.61 < stop_loss_price 253.50; last reviewed 2026-05-11T22:09. Either the desk-review loop missed today's bar or stop levels are misconfigured (stop set above entry of 248.99). |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **23 stuck PENDING `decision_points`** | Oldest: APA (id 1, 2026-04-09 — **806h / 33 days old**), TPL, PBMRF, DOCN, NXT, ASTS, SNGNF, ASND, QXO/PB, FIG×2, SSNC, BKRKY, CAR×2, IBRX, FORM, IMO, CNA, FN×2, COKE, G, SHOP (yesterday). These never advanced to a verdict. |
| 🔴 **HIGH** | **`ai_score` essentially unused** (398/408 NULL = 97.5%; only **1** row in the last 14 days has it set: id 10 OVV at 50.0) | Either the score is no longer being computed or the writer was removed. The downstream UI/filters that depend on it are running blind. |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | **DR funnel coverage gap on BUY_LIMIT** | 35 of 56 lifetime BUY_LIMIT rows (62.5%) never received a DR verdict. Last 14d: 23 BUY_LIMIT + 4 BUY = **27 BUY-tier rows skipped DR**. DR's senior-reviewer override is silently bypassed for most BUY_LIMITs. |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | **`sector` column 98.3% NULL** (401/408; only 7 set lifetime, 2 in last 14d) | All of today's 12 rows have NULL sector — sector-grouping in reports is currently impossible without inferring from `company_name`. |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | **9 test/synthetic rows in `decision_points`** | ids 367–375 with symbols `TEST`, `TEST_T3..T12`, `T8_BUY`, `T8_BUY_LIMIT`, `T8_WATCH`, `T8_AVOID` dated 2026-05-09. Three are marked `status='Owned'`. They distort lifetime BUY/PENDING counts and funnel rates. |
| 🟡 **MEDIUM** | **`decision_points.status='Owned'` rows with no `desk_positions` row** | 40 rows total. Today's ACM is the freshest case (legitimate timing lag possible); but several older rows (UI 05-08, SYM 05-08, FIS 05-08, EMBJ 05-08 …) and many April rows are inconsistent — likely the desk-position writer was rebuilt and not back-filled, OR the `status` flag is a leftover from a legacy code path. |
| 🟢 **LOW** | **DR `ERROR_PARSING` / `INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS` rows** | 4 lifetime: GOLF (2026-05-06, INCOMPLETE), ADBE (04-23), PPERF (04-11), HQY (04-10). Low rate (~5% of DR runs), but the GOLF one is recent enough to be worth re-running. |
| 🟢 **LOW** | **Today's screening volume below baseline** (12 vs avg 17.8) | Within historical noise band (min 4, max 24 over last 14d), so flag is informational rather than alarming. |
| 🟢 **LOW** | **`other` DR action: WATCH = 1, UPGRADED = 1, CONFIRMED = 1** | Rare verdict paths — single-sample, not actionable yet but worth watching that the parser handles them. |

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The strategy has a real edge but a small one.** Equal-weighted +1.22% per closed trade over 38 trades and a 55.3% win rate is positive — but the +6.28% TP winners are doing all the heavy lifting; without those 15 trades the rest of the book is net negative. Anything that reduces TP-hit rate will flip P&L.
- **Deep Research is earning its keep — when it runs.** 35.6% of DR runs overturn the PM, and today's ONON kill (governance + insider selling) is a textbook example. The blocker is coverage, not quality.
- **The May-6 industrials/chemicals batch is the worst single day of the lifetime.** CE −9.15%, NICE −8.74%, ALC −6.74%, ANET −3.75%. Four entries on one date, three stop-outs in 48h. Either the gatekeeper let too many correlated names through, or that day's market regime check (SPY/SMA200) should have suppressed the cohort.
- **DESK_DECISION exits look unprincipled.** 17 trades, avg −0.72%. Manual reviewers are pulling the trigger early on both winners and losers (range −3.75% to +3.80%). Either give them a tighter rule or let TP/SL/TS handle it.
- **The data layer is leaking.** `decision_tracking` empty, `ai_score` essentially gone, `sector` essentially gone, 23 stale PENDINGs going back 33 days — these are not isolated bugs, they're three or four background writers that have silently stopped. Without `decision_tracking` you cannot run the backtesting harness listed as the #1 backlog item in `CLAUDE.md`.
- **Test rows polluting production.** The 9 `TEST*` / `T8_*` rows from 2026-05-09 are inflating BUY/PENDING counts and the "Owned but no desk_position" mismatch. They should be deleted, not just hidden.
- **Today is a quiet day, narrowly filtered.** 12 candidates, 11 deep-dips, only ACM survived end-to-end. That's the pipeline doing its job — but it also means there is a single new live position to monitor, and tomorrow it's worth confirming the desk_positions row materializes.
- **WTW is the most pressing risk** and may actually be a non-issue caused by an inverted stop level — but it should be confirmed live before the next session, because as written, the system claims a position is alive that should have already exited.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions

Ordered by impact.

1. **Investigate the empty `decision_tracking` table** (HIGH). This blocks the backtesting harness — the #1 item in the backlog. Confirm whether the tracking_service worker is running, what cadence it's scheduled at, and whether it ever ran successfully in this DB. Restore writes before doing anything else.
2. **Resolve WTW immediately** (HIGH). Either close the position (current is below stop) or correct the stop_loss_price if the level was set in error. Audit `desk_positions` for any other rows where `current_price < stop_loss_price` while `status='ACTIVE'`.
3. **Bring DR coverage on BUY_LIMIT up to the BUY level** (HIGH). 23 of the last 14 days' BUY_LIMITs skipped DR. Either change the queueing rule so BUY_LIMITs always reach the senior reviewer, or document why they're exempt.
4. **Clear stale PENDING rows** (HIGH). 23 rows, oldest 33 days. Either re-run them through the pipeline or mark them ABANDONED so they stop polluting the funnel.
5. **Restore `ai_score` writes** (HIGH). 1/208 rows in the last 14 days has a score. If the field is deprecated, drop the column or stop displaying it. If it's still load-bearing, fix the writer.
6. **Delete the 9 `TEST*` / `T8_*` rows** (MEDIUM). They are inflating BUY/PENDING counts and creating phantom "Owned but no desk_position" mismatches.
7. **Re-back-fill `sector`** (MEDIUM). 98.3% NULL across history. Either revive the populating call, or run a one-off backfill from `company_name → sector` so analytics and reporting work.
8. **Re-run the 1 INCOMPLETE_TRADING_LEVELS DR for GOLF** (LOW). Recent, single, and now-closed-profitable — would let you confirm the parser fix.
9. **Post-mortem the 2026-05-06 industrials cluster** (MEDIUM). Three correlated losers from a single batch is the largest single-day drawdown in the book. Worth checking whether the gatekeeper's regime check fired, and whether batch correlation should be a new filter.
10. **Audit the 40 `decision_points.status='Owned'` rows with no matching `desk_positions`** (MEDIUM). Either back-fill the position rows or reset their `status` so the join is honest.
